D1 Optics L3 Apply Reference

Malus's Law

rotate a polariser between fixed and rotated and watch I = I₀ cos²θ

A polariser transmits only the component of the incoming electric field aligned with its axis — the rest is absorbed.

Slide θ across 360°. The beam between P1 and P2 stays full; after P2 it dims as cos²θ, reaching zero when the axes cross.
